pain_point_label: No Systematic Accommodations for Students with Learning Differences

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators lack differentiated assessment materials — alternate rubrics, modified question formats, extended-time instructions, and IEP-aligned versions of existing assessments. Text generation can produce these variants at scale from a base assessment, reducing the per-teacher burden that currently prevents systematic accommodation. This directly addresses the 'no alternate assessment formats' and 'no modified rubrics' pain mechanisms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool where teachers upload an existing assessment and select an accommodation profile (ELL, dyslexia, IEP-extended time, etc.) and receive a modified version — alternate phrasing, simplified language, scaffolded instructions — ready to distribute.

matches.match_rationale: Systematic identification of students who may have undiagnosed learning differences is a core gap in this pain point. Structured student data — attendance patterns, grade trajectories, support service usage, teacher flags — can be used to classify students into risk tiers for learning differences, enabling proactive referral rather than reactive identification. This is technically feasible given that multi-site operators already collect this data in SIS/LMS systems.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A screening layer integrated with the SIS that flags students exhibiting patterns consistent with undiagnosed learning differences, generating a referral recommendation for the learning support team.

matches.match_rationale: IEP and accommodation data often exists in unstructured or inconsistently formatted documents across a multi-site operator — PDFs, Word docs, or handwritten notes — making it impossible to systematically apply accommodations at assessment time. Transformation tooling can normalize accommodation records into a structured schema that integrates with grading and assessment workflows, directly addressing the 'assessments not aligned to IEP/accommodations' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ingestion pipeline that reads existing IEP documents and accommodation letters and outputs a standardized accommodation profile per student, fed into the assessment and grading system.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end accommodation workflow — identifying a student's accommodation needs, retrieving their IEP profile, generating a modified assessment, routing it to the right teacher, and logging compliance — is a multi-step process that no single capability handles alone. A task automation agent can orchestrate this entire workflow, connecting SIS data, document generation, and teacher notification in a repeatable pipeline. This directly addresses the systematic gap: current failures happen because no one has automated the coordination, not because teachers are unwilling.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An accommodation workflow agent that triggers when an assessment is created, checks enrolled students against accommodation records, auto-generates modified versions for flagged students, and delivers them to teachers with a compliance log.
